data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output path, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means the file is generated before compilation, but MSBuild has already collected the list of files to compile before this target runs.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written, inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects items to compile before custom targets run, so the generated file was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
